title: DuckDB C interface cannot correctly obtain the UUID result
description: ### What happens? In the duckdb.h provided by DuckDB, you can see that the UUID type is actually stored using duckdb_hugeint. However, when I tried to retrieve the content corresponding to the UUID using duckdb_value_hugeint, I encountered some issues with the internal implementation. In DuckDB, when C is used, GetInternalCValue is employed, and its internal implementation does not account for scenarios where the type obtained is a UUID. As a result, it resorts to fetching the default value, ultimately leading to the return of an all-zero value. It seems that a fix could be implemented by referencing the GetValuesInternal method. ### To Reproduce You can directly look at the code analysis above ### OS: x86_64 ### DuckDB Version: v1.1.1 ### DuckDB Client: C ### Hardware: _No response_ ### Full Name: Amore ### Affiliation: Software engineer ### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build. I have tested with a source build ### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue? Not applicable - the reproduction does not require a data set ### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue? - [X] Yes, I have ### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue? - [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_uuid (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, uuid_col TEXT NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_uuid (id, uuid_col) VALUES (1, hex(randomblob(16))), (2, hex(randomblob(16))), (3, hex(randomblob(16)));
SELECT id, uuid_col FROM test_uuid WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE test_uuid SET uuid_col = hex(randomblob(16)) WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, COALESCE(uuid_col, hex(randomblob(16))) AS new_uuid FROM test_uuid;
key_bug_features: C interface fails to retrieve UUID values correctly
